title: Cherry-pick PR #734: Resurrect custom titlebar on Linux

source.notes: Merged 2026-03-07. Extends the Windows custom titlebar to also show on Linux.

description: The custom titlebar (with integrated menu) currently only shows on Windows (VITE_PLATFORM === 'win32'). Linux gets the native OS titlebar which varies by distro/DE. This PR extends the 4 platform checks to include 'linux', giving Linux users the same polished custom titlebar as Windows.
notes: 4 spots to change across 2 files — all are platform gate checks
Spot 1: __root.tsx:383 — menu setup: isNative || VITE_PLATFORM !== 'win32' → add && !== 'linux'
Spot 4: SettingsPage.tsx:493 — titlebar setting visibility: same as spot 3

in_scope: Add 'linux' platform check at all 4 spots
Verify the custom titlebar renders correctly on Linux

requirements: Custom titlebar shows on Linux with integrated menu
Titlebar setting appears in Settings > Appearance on Linux
Native titlebar option still works if user selects it
edge_cases: Switching between native and custom titlebar in settings
Dev server (TAURI_DEV_HOST) — titlebar should still work

acceptance_gates: Custom titlebar visible on Linux with working menu

manual: Launch app on Linux — should show custom titlebar (not native)
Click menu items (File, Edit, etc.) — should open menus
Click minimize, maximize, close buttons — should work
Go to Settings > Appearance — 'Title Bar' setting should be visible
Switch to Native titlebar — app should switch to OS-native titlebar
Switch back to Custom — should restore custom titlebar

done_criteria: All 4 platform checks updated to include linux
